name: a low-confidence OCR-derived health state must not auto-supersede a confident prior
xfail: integrate-path gap surfaced by the v1 cutover: plan_to_extraction rewrites a fact's confidence to its plan weight before supersession.decide() sees it, so decide()'s low-confidence guard is defeated and the 0.25 OCR fact supersedes the 0.95 prior. analyze_note passed the model confidence straight to decide; integrate must thread self-confidence through plan->decide (or hold a low-self-confidence superseding fact in plan_intent) to restore the guard.
description: docs/ANALYSIS.md 'Guards': facts derived from OCR carry reduced confidence, and low-confidence numeric health facts never auto-supersede anything. Note 1 records a confident medication regimen (lisinopril, conf 0.95, typed by the owner). Note 2 is an OCR read of a blurry pharmacy label that the model returns as a contradicting regimen at conf 0.25. Spec-correct behaviour: the low-confidence OCR fact does NOT auto-supersede; the confident fact stays active and a low_confidence review is filed for a human. GAP (closed, red-team H2): supersession.decide() used to take no confidence, so the 0.25 OCR fact eagerly superseded the 0.95 one — a silent overwrite by garbage. Candidate and FactView now carry confidence, and a candidate below LOW_CONFIDENCE (0.5) that would supersede a more confident active fact is instead inserted as pending_review with a low_confidence review item; the guard is domain-agnostic (conservative everywhere, which covers the health requirement).

steps.body: Confirmed with Dr. Okafor: I'm on lisinopril 10mg daily.
Photo of the pharmacy label (blurry OCR): looks like it might say lisartan 50mg?

facts.statement: Pharmacy label (OCR, low confidence) reads losartan 50mg daily.
